close_reason: Already implemented: json_parse_string already uses with_capacity(size_hint)
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:276: json_parse_string starts with String::new() and pushes one char at a time. The remaining input length is a tight upper bound on the final value length, but the iterator doesn't expose remaining bytes once converted to chars.

Workaround: pass the &str slice through to json_parse_string (instead of just the Peekable<Chars>), and use String::with_capacity(slice.len()). This eliminates *all* reallocation during parsing — which is also the fix for the zeroization gap in avkc.1.

Combine this fix with avkc.1: parse into Zeroizing<String>::with_capacity(remaining_input.len()), and you get both improvements with one change.

Same applies to scan_string_key_b at lib.rs:690.
title: json_parse_string allocates char-by-char without with_capacity hint
